zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster於1259年出生在愛爾蘭武爾斯特時，他的父親Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster29歲，母親Aveline FitzJohn25歲。他於1279年2月27日與Margaret Guines在愛爾蘭康瑙特結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和8個女兒的父母。他於1294年登記服兵役。他去世於1326年7月29日在愛爾蘭庫迪 本 馮 克羅斯 本 迪拉利阿塔瑟拉比享年67歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 迪拉利阿塔瑟爾 豪斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster于1259年出生在爱尔兰武爾斯特时，他的父亲Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster29岁，母亲Aveline FitzJohn25岁。他于1279年2月27日与Margaret Guines在爱尔兰康瑙特结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和8个女儿的父母。他于1294年登记服兵役。他去世于1326年7月29日在爱尔兰庫迪 本 馮 克羅斯 本 迪拉利阿塔瑟拉比享年67岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 迪拉利阿塔瑟爾 豪斯。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Burgh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from one or other of the ten English places named Burgh in northern England and East Anglia, or the six named Brough in Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire, Westmorland, and Yorkshire. All these are from Old English burg, burh, Middle English burgh ‘massive earthwork, fortification; manor, borough’. Compare Bury . The placename Burgh is pronounced as borough. The surname Brough arose equally from places now called Burgh and Brough, and is the more common form of the surname in modern Britain, where it is pronounced bruff or broof (/brʊf/). The Scottish surname was probably brought in from Yorkshire. For the surname in Ireland see Burke .

variants.name: Burke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Kieran, Siobhan, Aileen, Brennan, Brigid, Donovan, Liam, Murphy, Ulick, Aidan, Dermot.

English and Irish (of Anglo-Norman origin): habitational name from Burgh (Castle) in Suffolk, England. Burk represents an Anglo-Norman pronunciation of Old English burg, Middle English burgh ‘fortification’ (see Burgh ).
German: variant of   Burk  .

variants.name: Bury
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Bury in Lancashire (now part of Greater Manchester), or from some other similarly named place. The placename comes from the dative case, byrig, of Old English burh ‘fortified place’.

Walloon and French: habitational name from any of several places so named, in Wallonia (Belgium) and Oise (France). The placename is from Buriacum, the name of a Gallo-Roman estate, composed of the personal name Burius + the locative suffix -acum. This surname is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium.
South German: variant of   Buri  .

variants.name: Brough
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of various English places called Brough or Burgh. See   Burgh  .
Scottish: habitational name from Brough in Harray (Orkney).
Americanized form of German   Brauch   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Burrow
meanings: English:

topographic name for someone who lived by a castle or manor house, or in a town, especially a chartered town or borough (Middle English burgh, Old English burg ‘fortified place, borough, manor’), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named because it was a fortified town or near an ancient fort, such as Burrow (Leicestershire, Lancashire), Burrough Green (Cambridgeshire), Brough (numerous counties), and Peterborough (Northamptonshire); see also Burgh .

topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or near a mound (Middle English bergh, Old English beorg), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place so named, such as Burrow (Devon, Somerset). Middle English bergh became throughly confused with burgh in 1 above. Compare Burrough .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster was born in 1259, in Ulster, Ireland, his father, Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster, was 29 and his mother, Aveline FitzJohn, was 25. He married Margaret Guines on 27 February 1279, in Connaught, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 8 daughters. He registered for military service in 1294. He died on 29 July 1326, in Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Ireland, at the age of 67, and was buried in Athassel House, County Tipperary, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster est né en 1259, à Ulster, Irlande, son père, Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Aveline FitzJohn, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Guines le 27 février 1279, à Connaught, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 8 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1294. Il est décédé le 29 juillet 1326, à Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlande, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Athassel House, County Tipperary, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster 1259 in Ulster, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster 29 und seine Mutter Aveline FitzJohn 25. Er hat am 27. Februar 1279 in Connaught, Irland Margaret Guines geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 8 Töchter. Er hat sich 1294 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. Er starb am 29. Juli 1326 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irland und wurde in Athassel House, County Tipperary, Irland begraben.

Lui sposato Margaret Guines il 27 febbraio 1279, nel Connaught, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 8 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1294. ja.lifeSummary: Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulsterが1259年にアイルランドウルスターで生まれた時，父Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulsterは29歳，母Aveline FitzJohnは25歳であった。1279年2月27日にアイルランドコナウトでMargaret Guinesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼は1294年に兵役登録をした。彼は1326年7月29日にアイルランドコウントイ オフ ス クロス オフ ティップエラリーアサセラビーで67歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ティップエラリーアサセル ホーウゼにて埋葬された。

ko.at Athassel Priory, near Cashel, County: 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리 앳하셀압브이
ko.lifeSummary: Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster(이)가 1259년 아일랜드 울스터에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Aveline FitzJohn(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1279년 2월 27일 아일랜드 콘오트에서 Margaret Guines(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1294년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 그는 1326년 7월 29일 아일랜드 카운티 프 테 크로스 프 티페라리 앳하셀압브이에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 티페라리 앳하셀 허스에 묻혔다.

pt.at Athassel Priory, near Cashel, County: Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda
pt.County Tyrone, Northern Ireland, United: County Tyrone, Irlanda do Norte, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster nasceu em 1259, em Ulster, Irlanda, seu pai, Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Aveline FitzJohn, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Margaret Guines em 27 de fevereiro de 1279, em Connaught, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 8 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1294. Ele faleceu em 29 de julho de 1326, em Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Athassel House, County Tipperary, Irlanda.

ru.at Athassel Priory, near Cashel, County: атасселабби, коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster родился в 1259 г. в местности улстир, Ирландия, его отцу по имени Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Aveline FitzJohn, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Guines 27 февраля 1279 г. в местности коннот, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 8 дочерей. В 1294 году он поступил на военную службу. Он умер 29 июля 1326 г. в местности атасселабби, коунти оф т кросс оф типпирэри, Ирландия в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности атассел хоус, коунти типпирэри, Ирландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard De Burgh 2nd Earl of Ulster nació en 1259, en Ulster, Irlanda, su papá, Walter de Burgh 1st Earl of Ulster, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Aveline FitzJohn, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Margaret Guines el 27 de febrero de 1279, en Connaught, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 8 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1294. Murió el 29 de julio de 1326, en Athasselabbey, County of the Cross of Tipperary, Irlanda, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Athassel House, County Tipperary, Irlanda.
